title: AWS HealthLake Vocabulary
description: Domain vocabulary and terminology for the AWS HealthLake API, covering FHIR R4, healthcare interoperability, and AWS service concepts.

terms.definition: A standard for health care data exchange, published by HL7. HealthLake implements the FHIR R4 (version 4.0.1) specification.
FHIR Release 4, the version supported by AWS HealthLake. R4 is the first normative release of the FHIR standard and the version required for US regulatory compliance.

An AWS HealthLake resource that represents a managed FHIR R4 server. Each data store has a unique endpoint for CRUD operations and bulk import/export. Also referred to as a FHIR data store or datastore.
A unique identifier assigned to a HealthLake data store at creation time. Used to reference the data store in all management API calls.
The current lifecycle state of a HealthLake data store.

values.description: The data store is being provisioned.
The data store is operational and accepting FHIR requests.
The data store is being deleted.
The data store has been deleted.

terms.definition: A set of open specifications to integrate apps with electronic health records and health IT data. HealthLake supports SMART App Launch Framework v1 using an external identity provider or a Lambda-based custom authorizer.
An asynchronous operation (StartFHIRImportJob) that loads FHIR R4 resources from Amazon S3 NDJSON files into a HealthLake data store. Supports up to 1 million files and 5 TB total data per job.

An asynchronous operation (StartFHIRExportJob) that exports all FHIR R4 resources from a HealthLake data store to Amazon S3 in NDJSON format. Supports analytics-ready output for use with Amazon Athena.
A format for streaming structured data where each line is a valid JSON value. Used by HealthLake for both bulk import and export of FHIR resources.
Configuration for server-side encryption of a HealthLake data store. Supports AWS-owned KMS keys (MANAGED_KMS_KEY type) or customer-managed KMS keys (CUSTOMER_MANAGED_KMS_KEY type).
The encryption configuration applied to a HealthLake data store, wrapping the KmsEncryptionConfig.
Optional configuration to preload synthetic Synthea-generated patient data into a new HealthLake data store for development and testing purposes.
An open-source synthetic patient and population health data simulator. HealthLake can optionally preload Synthea-generated FHIR R4 data into a new data store.
Configuration for SMART on FHIR authorization of a HealthLake data store. Supports SMART_ON_FHIR_V1 authorization strategy with optional Lambda-based fine-grained authorization.
Configuration for the Amazon S3 location and KMS key used for import/export jobs.
Specifies the source Amazon S3 location for a FHIR bulk import job.
Specifies the destination Amazon S3 location for a FHIR bulk export job.
The current state of an import or export job.

values.description: The job has been submitted but not yet started.
The job completed but some resources had errors.
A cancellation request has been submitted.

terms.abbreviation: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act
terms.definition: US legislation protecting patient health information. AWS HealthLake is a HIPAA-eligible service and can be used to store protected health information (PHI) when a Business Associate Agreement (BAA) is in place.
The US Core Implementation Guide, a set of FHIR profiles and value sets meeting the US Core Data for Interoperability (USCDI) requirements. HealthLake supports US Core IG.

terms.abbreviation: United States Core Data for Interoperability
terms.definition: A standardized set of health data classes and constituent data elements required for nationwide health information exchange. HealthLake supports USCDI via the US Core IG.
The process for adding authentication information to AWS API requests. All HealthLake management API requests must be signed with SigV4 using AWS IAM credentials.
An Amazon Resource Name (ARN) identifying an IAM role. Used in HealthLake import/export jobs to grant the service access to S3 resources.

An idempotency token provided by the client to ensure that a request is not processed more than once. HealthLake uses client tokens for CreateFHIRDatastore, StartFHIRImportJob, and StartFHIRExportJob.
A key-value pair attached to a HealthLake data store for cost allocation, access control, and resource management. Each data store supports up to 200 tags.
A FHIR mechanism for receiving notifications when resources change. HealthLake implements FHIR Subscriptions via Amazon EventBridge (channel type rest-hook).

AWS HealthLake includes AWS Comprehend Medical integration for extracting structured clinical data from unstructured text (clinical notes, discharge summaries, etc.) as part of the data enrichment pipeline.
An interactive query service used with HealthLake export data. HealthLake exports FHIR resources to S3 in analytics-ready format consumable by Amazon Athena for SQL-based querying.
An AWS PrivateLink interface endpoint that allows HealthLake API calls to remain within the AWS network without traversing the public internet.
Details about an error encountered during a HealthLake import or export job, including the error code and message.
